selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":78,"biological_sex":"female","gender":"non-binary","sexual_orientation":"homosexual","occupation":"waiter/waitress","city_of_living":"Bari","employment_status":"employed part-time","education_level":"master's degree","parents_education":{"parent_1":"vocational diploma","parent_2":"no formal education"},"marital_status":"married","children":4,"migration_status":"native Italian","psychological_feature":"normal","religious_beliefs":"Islam","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend several hours daily on social media","hobbies":["cooking","reading novels","football (calcio)","painting","yoga"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":66,"level":"moderate"},"conscientiousness":{"score":23,"level":"low"},"extraversion":{"score":77,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":99,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":47,"level":"moderate"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

mode = human
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = {"age":78,"biological_sex":"female","gender":"non-binary","sexual_orientation":"homosexual","occupation":"waiter/waitress","city_of_living":"Bari","employment_status":"employed part-time","education_level":"master's degree","parents_education":{"parent_1":"vocational diploma","parent_2":"no formal education"},"marital_status":"married","children":4,"migration_status":"native Italian","psychological_feature":"normal","religious_beliefs":"Islam","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend several hours daily on social media","hobbies":["cooking","reading novels","football (calcio)","painting","yoga"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":66,"level":"moderate"},"conscientiousness":{"score":23,"level":"low"},"extraversion":{"score":77,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":99,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":47,"level":"moderate"}}}

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "I am a non-binary Muslim woman living in Bari, Italy. I have a master's degree but still work part-time as a waiter/ waitress while managing my household and caring for my family. Social media is something I engage with regularly, spending several hours daily scrolling through feeds that mix real life stories with so much fake news about everything from cooking recipes gone wrong to political scandals spread carelessly.\n\nI remember reading an interesting article once in \"Il Giornale di Sicilia\" newspaper online how people can protect themselves. It said we need media literacy, which I think is very important especially for someone like me who balances university life with family responsibilities. My children love football but also learn about verifying information from their school textbooks that my husband helped prepare.\n\nAnother idea came from observing tourists in Bari – they always ask questions when unsure! So perhaps we should encourage critical thinking by asking ourselves: Who benefits if this is true? What evidence supports it?",
  "reasoning_summary": "I started with personal experience, then moved to external sources like a local newspaper and academic examples related to my children's education. I considered psychological traits that might affect susceptibility – agreeableness makes me want harmony but low conscientiousness means some carelessness in verifying details.",
  "tone": "Reflective yet concerned",
  "citations": ["Il Giornale di Sicilia"],
  "used_persona_fields":["age","education_level","hobbies","marital_status","children","ocean.conscientiousness"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I am a non-binary Muslim woman living in Bari, Italy. I have a master's degree but still work part-time as a waiter/ waitress while managing my household and caring for my family. Social media is something I engage with regularly, spending several hours daily scrolling through feeds that mix real life stories with so much fake news about everything from cooking recipes gone wrong to political scandals spread carelessly.

I remember reading an interesting article once in "Il Giornale di Sicilia" newspaper online how people can protect themselves. It said we need media literacy, which I think is very important especially for someone like me who balances university life with family responsibilities. My children love football but also learn about verifying information from their school textbooks that my husband helped prepare.

Another idea came from observing tourists in Bari – they always ask questions when unsure! So perhaps we should encourage critical thinking by asking ourselves: Who benefits if this is true? What evidence supports it?
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I started with personal experience, then moved to external sources like a local newspaper and academic examples related to my children's education. I considered psychological traits that might affect susceptibility – agreeableness makes me want harmony but low conscientiousness means some carelessness in verifying details.
